description: Malformed payload: event_type is missing entirely (no non-empty string event_type), so Workflow 1 Section C marks the whole payload MALFORMED, all 11 validated fields become null, and Section E1 proposes state REJECTED. Workflow 2 Section A then matches op-malformed (category OTHER) and Section C routes to terminal_status=REJECTED with stop_active_sequence=false (the sequence was never confirmed to exist for this event).

expected.notes: Even though raw_payload.lead_email and raw_payload.reply_text are present, Section C's isMalformed branch sets ALL 11 validated.* fields to null (not just event_type) once event_type is missing/invalid - this is intentional ('never fabricate values' / 'malformed payloads must route to a terminal rejected state, not continue silently'). Because validated.event_id/email_id/campaign_id/email_account and nes.lead_email ('UNKNOWN') are all null/UNKNOWN, Section E1 treats this payload as identifier-poor and computes idempotency_key='identifier_poor:instantly:payload_hash=<canonicalPayloadHash(raw_payload)>' (key_scheme='identifier_poor_hash') - a hash of this exact payload, not a fixed placeholder string. Fixture 13 is a second, content-different malformed payload; run_synthetic_tests.js's main loop cross-checks that fixtures 11 and 13 get DIFFERENT idempotency_key values and both independently reach op-malformed/REJECTED, resolving the shared-key collision previously logged in reports/UNRESOLVED_ITEMS.md U3.
